item.text: {"outcome":"fail","confidence":"Certain","findings":[{"check":"A-SEC-6","title":"Reset tokens use a non-cryptographic random source","severity":"High","path":"src/reset.js","line":6,"quote":"const token = `${Date.now().toString(36)}${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;"}],"reasoning":"All repository files were inspected. Password hashing uses bcrypt with cost 12 and verification uses bcrypt.compare. Reset-token digest comparison is length-checked and uses timingSafeEqual, and the password reset limiter is mounted before its route. However, reset tokens are generated with Date.now and Math.random rather than a CSPRNG, so A-SEC-6 fails."}
